citation.description: frm, to = (to_key, issue_key) if entry.get('swap') else (issue_key, to_key) — correctly swaps endpoints; test_link_sync.py:132-156 confirms both relations

criteria.criterion: Outbound apply honors swap_endpoints: a depends_on dep emits Jira 'B Blocks A' (swap --out/--in), while blocks is unchanged. Proof: unit test on the applier for both relations.

citation.description: test_update_one_swaps_endpoints_for_depends_on asserts set_relationship_calls == [(REB-2, REB-1, Blocks)] for swap=True depends_on entry

criteria.criterion: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_link_sync.py gains a depends_on (swap=True) case (currently only the blocks/swap=False path is tested).

citation.description: _diff_links_inbound correctly maps outwardIssue Blocks -> depends_on and deduplicates against existing_deps, preventing re-emission and cycle collisions; test_inbound_differ_reads_enriched_issuelinks (test_link_sync.py:211-237) validates the path

criteria.criterion: After the fix, an inbound pass over an A depends_on B pair applies idempotently (no cycle WARNING).

citation.description: Ticket comment records: live audit on 2026-07-16 of all 315 depends_on relationships via REST (GET /rest/api/3/issue/<key>?fields=issuelinks); result 315 correct / 0 reversed / 0 absent / 0 errors; spot-verified REB-1 and REB-832/30ff+siblings; no Jira mutation was needed or performed.
